creator: alt2600@icloud.com
text:
so I've been messing with this and have gotten past the assert failing by switching to gcc for 13.2 release and lower, haven't tested at all in 12. 

.if ${ARCH} != amd64 && ${ARCH} != i386
CMAKE_ARGS+=	-DHAVE_MMX:BOOL=OFF \
		-DHAVE_SSE:BOOL=OFF \
		-DHAVE_SSE2:BOOL=OFF
.endif

# switch to GCC for less than 13-stable
.if  ${OSVERSION}  <= 1302001
USE_GCC=13
.endif

so by adding this below the existing disabling mmx/sse if code I can get it to compile until it attempts to do this step in the build

[ 34% 401/1141] cd /usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/.build/libraries/lib-theme-resources && /usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/.build/Release/bin/image-compiler /usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/audacity-Audacity-3.4.2/libraries/lib-theme-resources classic
FAILED: /usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/audacity-Audacity-3.4.2/libraries/lib-theme-resources/ClassicThemeAsCeeCode.h 
cd /usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/.build/libraries/lib-theme-resources && /usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/.build/Release/bin/image-compiler /usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/audacity-Audacity-3.4.2/libraries/lib-theme-resources classic

with gcc12 image-compiler segfaults with a vtable issue in the libstdc++ runtime, but under gcc13 I get this with lldb:

lldb /usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/.build/Release/bin/image-compiler /usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/audacity-Audacity-3.4.2/libraries/lib-theme-resources classic
(lldb) target create "/usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/.build/Release/bin/image-compiler"
Current executable set to '/usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/.build/Release/bin/image-compiler' (x86_64).
(lldb) settings set -- target.run-args  "/usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/audacity-Audacity-3.4.2/libraries/lib-theme-resources" "classic"
(lldb) r
Process 57616 launched: '/usr/ports/audio/audacity/work/.build/Release/bin/image-compiler' (x86_64)
Process 57616 stopped
* thread #1, name = 'image-compiler', stop reason = signal SIGSEGV: invalid address (fault address: 0x80)
    frame #0: 0x00000008224cc102 lib-theme.so`ThemeBase::ReadImageCache(Identifier, bool) + 1858
lib-theme.so`ThemeBase::ReadImageCache:
->  0x8224cc102 <+1858>: movl   0x80(%rax), %edx
    0x8224cc108 <+1864>: movq   0x88(%rax), %rax
    0x8224cc10f <+1871>: movl   %edx, 0xd0(%rcx)
    0x8224cc115 <+1877>: movq   (%rax), %rsi
(lldb) bt
* thread #1, name = 'image-compiler', stop reason = signal SIGSEGV: invalid address (fault address: 0x80)
  * frame #0: 0x0000000822528102 lib-theme.so`ThemeBase::ReadImageCache(Identifier, bool) + 1858
    frame #1: 0x0000000822529066 lib-theme.so`ThemeBase::SwitchTheme(Identifier) + 422
    frame #2: 0x000000082252ca43 lib-theme.so`ThemeBase::LoadOneThemeComponents(Identifier, bool) + 131
    frame #3: 0x000000082252e23e lib-theme.so`ThemeBase::LoadThemeComponents(bool) + 142
    frame #4: 0x0000000000404d86 image-compiler`App::OnInit() + 102
    frame #5: 0x00000008255c9ae1 libwx_baseu-3.2.so.0`wxEntry(int&, wchar_t**) + 81
    frame #6: 0x0000000000404664 image-compiler`main + 1636
    frame #7: 0x00000000004048e0 image-compiler`_start(ap=<unavailable>, cleanup=<unavailable>) at crt1_c.c:75:7

I am about at the limit of my abilities, not sure how to proceed. but it seems gcc runtime might be viable if it can be worked out what is causing the segfault above, but then again it could be the underlying system runtime library still at issue. this occurs even after removing audacity from my system to ensure no tainted libraries come into the build. other odd thing is when gcc13 is selected it opts to use the -std=gnu++17 c++ version, not sure how to change that back to std c++20 . might try some more things, but I might give up and remove the port until i upgrade to 14 or 13.3 whichever makes more sense once at least the release has settled.

I did notice that for 13-stable LLVM had this new file https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-src/blob/stable/13/contrib/llvm-project/libcxx/include/__type_traits/conjunction.h which seems like it might be a merge between LLVM15 and 16 c++ libraries from what I gather in the repo. but again, this is starting to get above my knowledge base. audacity team points to buggy std::conjunction issues with some c++ libraries, seemingly LLVM.

maybe someone smarter then I would know if this is fixable, unless is this still that wx issue? maybe I just need those patches to try? can't seem to find them in bug posts anymore. not sure which pr it was
